own_review.issue: Fallback failures are caught as 'catch (Exception ignored)' and discarded entirely, unlike CredentialHelper and ProcessRunner which addSuppressed; the final RuntimeException carries neither cause nor suppressed exceptions, losing diagnostics and being inconsistent across the three sites.
An InterruptedException thrown by a fallback check (process.waitFor) is swallowed in the ignored-catch without restoring the interrupt flag.

The loop relies on processBuilder.command(command) mutating shared builder state between iterations; it is only correct because 'executable' is captured before the loop and set(0, ...) fully overwrites element 0. Fragile pattern; a fresh ProcessBuilder per attempt (as in the other two files) would be safer.

The new per-directory startsWith(binDirectory) guards are scope creep: helper/command names never contain '/', so the guards are unnecessary (matches the maintainer's view: revert). In CredentialHelper the guard is entirely new behavior unrelated to adding the homebrew fallback.
Test could also assert ex.getMessage() doesNotContain '/opt/homebrew/bin/' and that exactly 2 suppressed exceptions exist; anySatisfy alone is weaker than the old allSatisfy.

Behavioral change in failure reporting (improvement): the thrown ProcessStartException now always wraps the original command and original exception with retry failures suppressed, instead of the old recursion which reported the /usr/local/bin-prefixed command on retry failure. Verified base startProcess only set directory(), so the inline retry loses no ProcessBuilder configuration.
path.contains(binDirectory) is a loose substring match (e.g. '/usr/local/bind' matches); pre-existing pattern, not introduced by this PR.

points.point: CredentialHelper loop mutates processBuilder.command in-place but stays correct because executable is captured pre-loop; suggest fresh ProcessBuilder per attempt for consistency with the other two files
points.note: Verified accurate; matches my own finding #3 with correct mutation analysis.

points.point: Test input-shape inconsistency: production always passes bare 'docker-credential-' names (DockerRegistryConfigAuthentication:~59 verified) while other tests use geExecutableAbsolutePath, which the startsWith guard does not cover
points.note: All factual claims verified in repo, including the typo'd method name; engages the same guard the human flagged.

points.point: ProcessRunnerTests has no tests for the macOS fallback (neither /usr/local/bin nor /opt/homebrew/bin); runWhenProcessDoesNotStart does not assert suppressed exceptions
points.note: Verified: only 4 tests, none cover fallback.

points.point: DisabledIfProcessUnavailableTests is a single smoke test that does not exercise the fallback logic
points.note: Verified true, but unit-testing a JUnit condition's PATH fallback is impractical; soft coverage nit.

points.point: Trailing-slash constant inconsistency between CredentialHelper and the other two classes

missed: Human: DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition startsWith guard is unrelated/unnecessary, should be reverted
Human: ProcessRunner startsWith guard is unrelated/unnecessary, should be reverted
Own: DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition discards fallback exceptions without addSuppressed (inconsistent with the other two files, loses diagnostics)
exceeds: Verified cross-file evidence (DockerRegistryConfigAuthentication) for production input shape

points.point: Trailing-slash convention inconsistency across the three constants
startsWith guard misses non-standard absolute paths, producing compound paths like /opt/homebrew/bin//custom/path/...; suggests broader startsWith("/") guard
points.note: Accurate analysis of the guard the human also flagged, though it suggests strengthening rather than removing it.

points.point: Claims the ProcessRunner fallback 'calls startProcess(localCommand) recursively' and re-iterates MAC_OS_BIN_DIRECTORIES, recommending early return
points.note: Fabricated: the new code calls localProcessBuilder.start() directly; recursion existed only in the pre-PR code. The described redundant re-iteration cannot happen.

points.point: anySatisfy change correct; suggest hasSize(2) before anySatisfy
Codebase-wide verification that the three files are the complete set of macOS fallback sites; installing.adoc:157 still references /usr/local/bin for Homebrew
points.note: Both claims verified true in repo; useful completeness diligence. The adoc reference is about the spring CLI binary, so the doc suggestion is tangential but factually correct.

missed: Human: DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition startsWith guard is unrelated/unnecessary, should be reverted
Human: ProcessRunner startsWith guard is unrelated/unnecessary, should be reverted
Own: DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition discards fallback exceptions without addSuppressed (inconsistent with the other two files, loses diagnostics)
exceeds: Completeness scan confirming no other fallback sites

qualitative: This is a clean, low-risk PR whose only real maintainer objection was that the new startsWith(binDirectory) guards are unrelated scope creep to revert; neither candidate reached that conclusion, though both engaged substantively with the CredentialHelper guard, and both missed the new exception-swallowing inconsistency in DisabledIfProcessUnavailableCondition. v5 was fully grounded: every factual claim checked out against the repo, including the cross-file DockerRegistryConfigAuthentication evidence and two genuine test-coverage gaps. v6 produced one outright fabrication, describing a recursive startProcess call and redundant re-iteration that exist only in the pre-PR code, which undercuts an otherwise diligent review that correctly verified completeness across the codebase and a true documentation reference. Neither candidate made false absence claims; v6's absence claims (no other fallback sites) were verified true. Overall v5 edges out v6 on factual reliability.
